issues.comment: Wrong group_key in `TestEvaluateGroupKeyValue.test_dedupe` test. The expected occurrence is built with `group_key="val1"`, but `evaluate_group_key_value` is called with `"group_key"`, which means the actual result's occurrence is built via `self.build_occurrence_and_event_data("group_key", ...)`, producing a fingerprint for `"group_key"`. The expected occurrence has the wrong fingerprint (for `"val1"`). This is a copy-paste error from adjacent tests. The test still passes only because `IssueOccurrence.__eq__` compares only by `self.id == other.id` and both use the same hardcoded id, so the fingerprint mismatch is silently ignored, masking potential fingerprint bugs.

Wrong value for group_2 in `test_state_results_multi_group`. The data packet has `"group_2": 10`, but the expected occurrence is built with value `6` (copied from group_1). The production code calls `self.build_occurrence_and_event_data(group_key, value, ...)` with the actual value `10`. The test passes because `build_mock_occurrence_and_event` accepts a `value` parameter but never uses it anywhere in the function body — the value is silently discarded. This means the test doesn't validate that the correct value is propagated through the system.
